senators.name: Ted Cruz
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. summa cum laude (Phi Beta Kappa) from Princeton University and J.D. magna cum laude from Harvard Law School (editor of Harvard Law Review). Clerked for Chief Justice William Rehnquist. Exceptional academic credentials. However, was a key promoter of 2020 election fraud claims — a documented rejection of evidence — and has staked out anti-climate-consensus positions. Legislative behavior contradicts the analytical rigor his credentials would suggest.
education: J.D. (magna cum laude), Harvard Law School
B.A. (summa cum laude, Phi Beta Kappa), Princeton University
experience: Law Clerk to Chief Justice William Rehnquist

senators.name: John Cornyn
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. from Trinity University, J.D. from St. Mary's University School of Law, and LL.M. from University of Virginia School of Law. Career as Texas Supreme Court Justice, Attorney General, and senator. Three law degrees. Deep judicial and legal analytical expertise but governance approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: LL.M., University of Virginia School of Law
J.D., St. Mary's University School of Law

TX-01.name: Nathaniel Moran
TX-01.notNerdReason: B.A. from Hardin-Simmons University and J.D. from Baylor University School of Law. Career as attorney and Smith County Judge. Legal and judicial background provides analytical skills but governance approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., Baylor University School of Law

TX-02.name: Dan Crenshaw
TX-02.nerdReason: B.A. from Tufts University and M.S. in defense analysis from Harvard Kennedy School (international security). Navy SEAL officer who lost his eye to an IED in Afghanistan. Harvard Kennedy School master's in defense analysis demonstrates rigorous analytical engagement with security and foreign policy. Applies strategic thinking and defense research expertise to national security and defense policy.
education: M.S. Defense Analysis, Harvard Kennedy School
experience: U.S. Representative since 2019 (lost 2026 Republican primary to Steve Toth, serving through Jan 2027)
10 deployments including combat in Iraq and Afghanistan

TX-02.name: Steve Toth
TX-02.notNerdReason: Attended Rochester Bible College. Career as pool company owner, former pastor, and Texas state representative. No traditional academic credentials or analytical policy background. Won the March 2026 Republican primary defeating Crenshaw 56%-41%. Faces Democrat Shaun Finnie in November 2026 general election.
experience: Republican nominee, TX-02, November 2026 general election
Owner, Acclaim Pools and My Pool Xpert
Former Pastor, Fellowship of The Woodlands

TX-03.name: Keith Self
TX-03.notNerdReason: B.S. from U.S. Military Academy at West Point and M.A. from University of Texas at Dallas. Army Reserve officer. West Point provides strong analytical foundation. Career as Collin County Judge. Military and judicial background provides structured thinking but legislative approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: M.A., University of Texas at Dallas
B.S., U.S. Military Academy at West Point

TX-04.name: Pat Fallon
TX-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from Notre Dame. Career in Texas state legislature. Notre Dame education provides intellectual foundation but no analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

TX-05.name: Lance Gooden
TX-05.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Texas at Austin. Career in state legislature. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
education: B.A., University of Texas at Austin

TX-06.name: Jake Ellzey
TX-06.notNerdReason: B.S. from Emory University and M.S. from University of Texas Pan American. U.S. Navy pilot. Career as airline pilot, Texas state rep. Military flight training requires precision and analytical skills but legislative approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: M.S., University of Texas Pan American
experience: U.S. Navy and National Guard Pilot

TX-07.name: Lizzie Fletcher
TX-07.nerdReason: B.A. from Dartmouth College and J.D. from New York University School of Law. Career as attorney in complex commercial litigation. Dartmouth + NYU Law represents elite analytical education. Deep expertise in energy, healthcare, and technology policy. Known for methodical, evidence-based approach to legislation.
education: J.D., New York University School of Law

TX-08.name: Morgan Luttrell
TX-08.notNerdReason: B.A. from Sam Houston State University and M.S. from Texas A&M. Navy SEAL. Career as neuroscience-focused veteran advocate after sustaining traumatic brain injury. Has done advocacy work grounded in medical research on TBI. Military background provides analytical discipline but legislative record too limited.

TX-09.name: Al Green
TX-09.notNerdReason: B.A. from Florida A&M University and J.D. from Texas Southern University. Career as attorney, HUD administrator, and state legislator. Legal training provides analytical skills. Long-tenured member with deep expertise on civil rights and financial services. Approach is more advocacy-driven than analytically rigorous.

TX-10.name: Michael McCaul
TX-10.notNerdReason: B.A. from Trinity University and J.D. from St. Mary's University School of Law. Career as federal prosecutor and homeland security expert. Former Chairman of House Homeland Security Committee. Legal and national security analytical skills but governance approach not distinctly research-driven.
education: J.D., St. Mary's University School of Law
experience: Former Chair, House Foreign Affairs Committee
Former Chair, House Homeland Security Committee

TX-11.name: August Pfluger
TX-11.nerdReason: B.S. from U.S. Military Academy at West Point, M.S. from Naval Postgraduate School, and M.S. from National Intelligence University. Three degrees including specialized graduate work in intelligence and national security. Air Force fighter pilot and National Security Council senior director. Combines rigorous USMA education with intelligence analysis expertise at the highest level of government.
education: B.S., U.S. Military Academy at West Point

TX-12.name: Craig Goldman
TX-12.notNerdReason: Career as businessman and Texas state legislator. Replaced Kay Granger who retired. New member with limited public record of analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

TX-13.name: Ronny Jackson
TX-13.notNerdReason: B.S. from Texas A&M University and M.D. from University of Texas Health Science Center. Former White House physician to three presidents. Medical degree represents genuine scientific training. However, has spread medical misinformation, including false claims about COVID vaccines, contradicting evidence-based medicine. Clinical credentials do not match legislative behavior on health science.
education: M.D., University of Texas Health Science Center

TX-14.name: Randy Weber
TX-14.notNerdReason: B.S. from Lamar University. Career as air conditioning and refrigeration contractor. Has denied climate science consensus. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

TX-15.name: Monica De La Cruz
TX-15.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Texas Pan American. Career as businesswoman and insurance agent. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.
education: B.A., University of Texas Pan American

TX-16.name: Veronica Escobar
TX-16.notNerdReason: B.A. from UTEP and M.A. from University of Texas at Austin. Career as county judge and state legislator. Graduate degree and judicial career provide analytical training. Deep expertise in immigration and border policy. Solid credentials but approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: M.A., University of Texas at Austin

TX-17.name: Pete Sessions
TX-17.notNerdReason: B.A. from Southwestern University. Career at Southwestern Bell and in Congress. Business and political career. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

TX-18.name: Christian Menefee
TX-18.nerdReason: B.A. from Duke University and J.D. from University of Texas School of Law. Career as commercial litigator. Served as Harris County Attorney — youngest and first Black person in that role — applying legal and policy expertise to public health, environmental enforcement, and civil rights. Duke + UT Law demonstrates strong analytical legal training applied to public interest work.
education: J.D., University of Texas School of Law

TX-19.name: Jodey Arrington
TX-19.nerdReason: B.A., M.A., and Ph.D. all from Texas Tech University. Former President of the George W. Bush Institute — a policy research center. A doctorate plus leadership of a major policy research institution demonstrates deep scholarly and analytical engagement. Applies research-backed approaches to budget and agriculture policy as Chairman of the House Budget Committee.

TX-20.name: Joaquin Castro
TX-20.nerdReason: B.A. from Stanford University and J.D. from Harvard Law School. Career as attorney and state legislator. Twin brother of former HUD Secretary Julián Castro. Stanford + Harvard Law represents exceptional analytical academic training. Serves on House Intelligence and Armed Services committees. Applies legal analytical expertise to national security and foreign policy.

TX-21.name: Chip Roy
TX-21.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Virginia and J.D. from University of Texas School of Law. Career as Texas Attorney General chief of staff and congressional staffer. Legal background but legislative approach is aggressively ideological rather than evidence-based.
education: J.D., University of Texas School of Law
experience: Chief of Staff, Texas Attorney General

TX-22.name: Troy Nehls
TX-22.notNerdReason: B.S. from Chadron State College. Career as Fort Bend County Sheriff. Law enforcement background with no analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

TX-23.name: Tony Gonzales
TX-23.notNerdReason: B.S. from Wayland Baptist University and M.A. from American Military University. Career as Navy cryptologist. Military intelligence career provides some analytical training. Known for moderate positions on immigration but approach not distinctly evidence-based.

TX-24.name: Beth Van Duyne
TX-24.nerdReason: B.S. from Colgate University and M.P.P. from Harvard Kennedy School. Career as technology company executive and mayor of Irving, TX. Harvard Kennedy School provides elite policy analysis training. Applied data-driven governance to city management. Tech executive background adds analytical skills. Known for detailed policy work on housing and small business.

TX-25.name: Roger Williams
TX-25.notNerdReason: B.A. from Texas Christian University. Career as car dealership owner and Texas Secretary of State. Business background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

TX-36.nerdReason: B.S. from Lamar University and D.D.S. from University of Texas Health Science Center Houston. Practiced dentistry for over 20 years. TX-37.name: Lloyd Doggett
TX-37.nerdReason: B.A. summa cum laude from University of Texas at Austin and J.D. from University of Texas School of Law (editor, Texas Law Review). Former Texas Supreme Court Justice.
